content.problemStatement:
Chef, to entertain customers, decided to establish some kind of game in his restaurant. And here is what he invented:

n players sit around a round table. There is n * m cards with unique numbers of range **1..n*m**.

Each player has m cards. In each set of the game each player selects some card and everyone shows his card at the same time. Card of **ith** player is compared to card of player **p[i]**. If it’s number is greater than the number on card of player **p[i]**, then **ith** gets a point. **P** is a permutation of sequence **0..n-1**.

But all customers when playing this game want to win. Chef on his side wants to please his customers. So, he decided to give cards in such way, that each player gets a point with probability larger than **½**.

But it appears to be a complicated task, so he asks you for help.
constraints: p is a permutation of sequence 0..n-1
content.inputFormat: The first line contains the number of test cases T. Each test case consists of two lines. The first line contains two numbers n and m. The second line contains a permutation p of sequence 0..n-1.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, if there's no solution, output 'No Solution'. Otherwise, output n lines with cards for each player. The ith line should contain m numbers in increasing order - numbers on ith player's cards.

sampleTestCases.explanation: For the first test case, there is no solution because there is only one card. For the second test case, the output is a possible assignment of cards to players.
There is no solution for this test case because it is not possible to assign cards such that each player has a higher card than their opponent with a probability greater than 1/2.
There is no solution for this test case because it is not possible to assign cards such that each player has a higher card than their opponent with a probability greater than 1/2.

solution.approach: To solve this problem, we need to find a way to assign cards to players such that each player has a higher card than their opponent with a probability greater than 1/2. We can use a graph theory approach to model the problem and find a solution.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T * n * m) per test case, O(T * n * m) total.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(n * m) — the dominant structure is the assigned_cards 2D array.
